NKJV.1: What advantage then has the Jew, or what is the profit of circumcision?
NKJV.2: Much in every way! Chiefly because to them were committed the oracles of God.
NKJV.3: For what if some did not believe? Will their unbelief make the faithfulness of God without effect?
NKJV.4: Certainly not! Indeed, let God be true but every man a liar. As it is written:  “That You may be justified in Your words, And may overcome when You are judged.”
NKJV.5: But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unjust who inflicts wrath? (I speak as a man.)
NKJV.6: Certainly not! For then how will God judge the world?
NKJV.7: For if the truth of God has increased through my lie to His glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?
NKJV.8: And why not say, “Let us do evil that good may come”?—as we are slanderously reported and as some affirm that we say. Their condemnation is just.
NKJV.9: What then? Are we better than they? Not at all. For we have previously charged both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.
NKJV.10: As it is written:  “There is none righteous, no, not one;
NKJV.11: There is none who understands; There is none who seeks after God.
NKJV.12: They have all turned aside; They have together become unprofitable; There is none who does good, no, not one.”
NKJV.13: “Their throat is an open tomb; With their tongues they have practiced deceit”; “The poison of asps is under their lips”;
NKJV.14: “Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.”
NKJV.16: Destruction and misery are in their ways;
NKJV.17: And the way of peace they have not known.”
NKJV.18: “There  is no fear of God before their eyes.”
NKJV.19: Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
NKJV.20: Therefore by the deeds of the law no flesh will be justified in His sight, for by the law is the knowledge of sin.
NKJV.21: But now the righteousness of God apart from the law is revealed, being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets,
NKJV.22: even the righteousness of God, through faith in Jesus Christ, to all and on all who believe. For there is no difference;
NKJV.23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
NKJV.24: being justified freely by His grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
NKJV.25: whom God set forth as a propitiation by His blood, through faith, to demonstrate His righteousness, because in His forbearance God had passed over the sins that were previously committed,
NKJV.26: to demonstrate at the present time His righteousness, that He might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
NKJV.27: Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? Of works? No, but by the law of faith.
NKJV.28: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith apart from the deeds of the law.
NKJV.29: Or is He the God of the Jews only? Is He not also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also,
NKJV.30: since there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
NKJV.31: Do we then make void the law through faith? Certainly not! On the contrary, we establish the law.

ESV.1: Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
ESV.2: Much in every way. To begin with, the Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God.
ESV.3: What if some were unfaithful? Does their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
ESV.4: By no means! Let God be true though every one were a liar, as it is written, “That you may be justified in your words, and prevail when you are judged.”
ESV.5: But if our unrighteousness serves to show the righteousness of God, what shall we say? That God is unrighteous to inflict wrath on us? (I speak in a human way.)
ESV.6: By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
ESV.7: But if through my lie God's truth abounds to his glory, why am I still being condemned as a sinner?
ESV.8: And why not do evil that good may come? — as some people slanderously charge us with saying. Their condemnation is just.
ESV.9: What then? Are we Jews  any better off?  No, not at all. For we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks, are under sin,
ESV.10: as it is written: “None is righteous, no, not one;
ESV.11: no one understands; no one seeks for God.
ESV.12: All have turned aside; together they have become worthless; no one does good, not even one.”
ESV.13: “Their throat is an open grave; they use their tongues to deceive.” “The venom of asps is under their lips.”
ESV.14: “Their mouth is full of curses and bitterness.”
ESV.15: “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
ESV.16: in their paths are ruin and misery,
ESV.17: and the way of peace they have not known.”
ESV.18: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
ESV.19: Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be stopped, and the whole world may be held accountable to God.
ESV.20: For by works of the law no human being  will be justified in his sight, since through the law comes knowledge of sin.
ESV.21: But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law, although the Law and the Prophets bear witness to it —
ESV.22: the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction:
ESV.23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
ESV.24: and are justified by his grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
ESV.25: whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood, to be received by faith. This was to show God's righteousness, because in his divine forbearance he had passed over former sins.
ESV.26: It was to show his righteousness at the present time, so that he might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
ESV.27: Then what becomes of our boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? By a law of works? No, but by the law of faith.
ESV.28: For we hold that one is justified by faith apart from works of the law.
ESV.29: Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
ESV.30: since God is one — who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
ESV.31: Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary, we uphold the law.

NASB.1: Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the benefit of circumcision?
NASB.2: Great in every respect. First of all, that they were entrusted with the oracles of God.
NASB.3: What then? If some did not believe, their unbelief will not nullify the faithfulness of God, will it?
NASB.4: May it never be! Rather, let God be found true, though every man [be found] a liar, as it is written, 'THAT YOU MAY BE JUSTIFIED IN YOUR WORDS, AND PREVAIL WHEN YOU ARE JUDGED.'
NASB.5: But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God, what shall we say? The God who inflicts wrath is not unrighteous, is He? (I am speaking in human terms.)
NASB.6: May it never be! For otherwise, how will God judge the world?
NASB.7: But if through my lie the truth of God abounded to His glory, why am I also still being judged as a sinner?
NASB.8: And why not [say] (as we are slanderously reported and as some claim that we say), 'Let us do evil that good may come '? Their condemnation is just.
NASB.9: What then? Are we better than they? Not at all; for we have already charged that both Jews and Greeks are all under sin;
NASB.10: as it is written, 'THERE IS NONE RIGHTEOUS, NOT EVEN ONE;
NASB.11: THERE IS NONE WHO UNDERSTANDS, THERE IS NONE WHO SEEKS FOR GOD;
NASB.12: ALL HAVE TURNED ASIDE, TOGETHER THEY HAVE BECOME USELESS; THERE IS NONE WHO DOES GOOD, THERE IS NOT EVEN ONE.'
NASB.13: 'THEIR THROAT IS AN OPEN GRAVE, WITH THEIR TONGUES THEY KEEP DECEIVING,' 'THE POISON OF ASPS IS UNDER THEIR LIPS';
NASB.14: 'WHOSE MOUTH IS FULL OF CURSING AND BITTERNESS';
NASB.15: 'THEIR FEET ARE SWIFT TO SHED BLOOD,
NASB.16: DESTRUCTION AND MISERY ARE IN THEIR PATHS,
NASB.17: AND THE PATH OF PEACE THEY HAVE NOT KNOWN.'
NASB.18: 'THERE IS NO FEAR OF GOD BEFORE THEIR EYES.'
NASB.19: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, so that every mouth may be closed and all the world may become accountable to God;
NASB.20: because by the works of the Law no flesh will be justified in His sight; for through the Law [comes] the knowledge of sin.
NASB.21: But now apart from the Law [the] righteousness of God has been manifested, being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets,
NASB.22: even [the] righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all those who believe; for there is no distinction;
NASB.23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
NASB.24: being justified as a gift by His grace through the redemption which is in Christ Jesus;
NASB.25: whom God displayed publicly as a propitiation in His blood through faith. [This was] to demonstrate His righteousness, because in the forbearance of God He passed over the sins previously committed;
NASB.26: for the demonstration, [I say], of His righteousness at the present time, so that He would be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
NASB.27: Where then is boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? No, but by a law of faith.
NASB.28: For we maintain that a man is justified by faith apart from works of the Law.
NASB.29: Or is God [the God] of Jews only? Is He not [the God] of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
NASB.30: since indeed God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith is one.
NASB.31: Do we then nullify the Law through faith? May it never be! On the contrary, we establish the Law.

NLT.1: Then what’s the advantage of being a Jew? Is there any value in the ceremony of circumcision?
NLT.2: Yes, there are great benefits! First of all, the Jews were entrusted with the whole revelation of God.
NLT.3: True, some of them were unfaithful; but just because they were unfaithful, does that mean God will be unfaithful?
NLT.4:
Of course not! Even if everyone else is a liar, God is true. As the Scriptures say about him, “You will be proved right in what you say,
and you will win your case in court.”
NLT.5: “But,” some might say, “our sinfulness serves a good purpose, for it helps people see how righteous God is. Isn’t it unfair, then, for him to punish us?” (This is merely a human point of view.)
NLT.6: Of course not! If God were not entirely fair, how would he be qualified to judge the world?
NLT.7: “But,” someone might still argue, “how can God condemn me as a sinner if my dishonesty highlights his truthfulness and brings him more glory?”
NLT.8: And some people even slander us by claiming that we say, “The more we sin, the better it is!” Those who say such things deserve to be condemned.
NLT.9: Well then, should we conclude that we Jews are better than others? No, not at all, for we have already shown that all people, whether Jews or Gentiles, are under the power of sin.
NLT.10:
As the Scriptures say, “No one is righteous —
not even one.
NLT.11:
No one is truly wise;
no one is seeking God.
NLT.12:
All have turned away;
all have become useless.
No one does good,
not a single one.”
NLT.13:
“Their talk is foul, like the stench from an open grave.
Their tongues are filled with lies.”
“Snake venom drips from their lips.”
NLT.14: “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
NLT.16: Destruction and misery always follow them.
NLT.17: They don’t know where to find peace.”
NLT.18: “They have no fear of God at all.”
NLT.19: Obviously, the law applies to those to whom it was given, for its purpose is to keep people from having excuses, and to show that the entire world is guilty before God.
NLT.20: For no one can ever be made right with God by doing what the law commands. The law simply shows us how sinful we are.
NLT.21: But now God has shown us a way to be made right with him without keeping the requirements of the law, as was promised in the writings of Moses and the prophets long ago.
NLT.22: We are made right with God by placing our faith in Jesus Christ. And this is true for everyone who believes, no matter who we are.
NLT.23: For everyone has sinned; we all fall short of God’s glorious standard.
NLT.24: Yet God, in his grace, freely makes us right in his sight. He did this through Christ Jesus when he freed us from the penalty for our sins.
NLT.25: For God presented Jesus as the sacrifice for sin. People are made right with God when they believe that Jesus sacrificed his life, shedding his blood. This sacrifice shows that God was being fair when he held back and did not punish those who sinned in times past,
NLT.26: for he was looking ahead and including them in what he would do in this present time. God did this to demonstrate his righteousness, for he himself is fair and just, and he makes sinners right in his sight when they believe in Jesus.
NLT.27: Can we boast, then, that we have done anything to be accepted by God? No, because our acquittal is not based on obeying the law. It is based on faith.
NLT.28: So we are made right with God through faith and not by obeying the law.
NLT.29: After all, is God the God of the Jews only? Isn’t he also the God of the Gentiles? Of course he is.
NLT.30: There is only one God, and he makes people right with himself only by faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
NLT.31: Well then, if we emphasize faith, does this mean that we can forget about the law? Of course not! In fact, only when we have faith do we truly fulfill the law.

WEB.1: Then what advantage does the Jew have? Or what is the profit of circumcision?
WEB.2: Much in every way! Because first of all, they were entrusted with the revelations of God.
WEB.3: For what if some were without faith? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God?
WEB.4: May it never be! Yes, let God be found true, but every man a liar. As it is written, “That you might be justified in your words, and might prevail when you come into judgment.”
WEB.5: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what will we say? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath? I speak like men do.
WEB.6: May it never be! For then how will God judge the world?
WEB.7: For if the truth of God through my lie abounded to his glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?
WEB.8: Why not (as we are slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say), “Let us do evil, that good may come?” Those who say so are justly condemned.
WEB.9: What then? Are we better than they? No, in no way. For we previously warned both Jews and Greeks, that they are all under sin.
WEB.10: As it is written, “There is no one righteous; no, not one.
WEB.11: There is no one who understands. There is no one who seeks after God.
WEB.12: They have all turned aside. They have together become unprofitable. There is no one who does good, no, not so much as one.”
WEB.13: “Their throat is an open tomb. With their tongues they have used deceit.”  “The poison of vipers is under their lips”;
WEB.14: “whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.”
WEB.15: “Their feet are swift to shed blood.
WEB.16: Destruction and misery are in their ways.
WEB.17: The way of peace, they haven’t known.”
WEB.18: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
WEB.19: Now we know that whatever things the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be closed, and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God.
WEB.20: Because by the works of the law, no flesh will be justified in his sight. For through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
WEB.21: But now apart from the law, a righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified by the law and the prophets;
WEB.22: even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all and on all those who believe. For there is no distinction,
WEB.23: for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God;
WEB.24: being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus;
WEB.25: whom God sent to be an atoning sacrifice,  through faith in his blood, for a demonstration of his righteousness through the passing over of prior sins, in God’s forbearance;
WEB.26: to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time; that he might himself be just, and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus.
WEB.27: Where then is the boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? No, but by a law of faith.
WEB.28: We maintain therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the works of the law.
WEB.29: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isn’t he the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
WEB.30: since indeed there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith, and the uncircumcised through faith.
WEB.31: Do we then nullify the law through faith? May it never be! No, we establish the law.

CSB17.1: So what advantage does the Jew have? Or what is the benefit of circumcision?
CSB17.2: Considerable in every way. First, they were entrusted <sup>ⓛ</sup> with the very words of God. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: What then? If some were unfaithful, <sup>ⓝ</sup> will their unfaithfulness nullify God’s faithfulness?
CSB17.4:
Absolutely not! ⓐ Let God be true, even though everyone is a liar, ⓑ as it is written: That you may be justified in your words
and triumph when you judge. [1] ⓒ
CSB17.5: But if our unrighteousness highlights <sup>[2]</sup> God’s righteousness, <sup>ⓓ</sup> what are we to say? <sup>ⓔ</sup> I am using a human argument: <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> Is God unrighteous to inflict wrath?
CSB17.6: Absolutely not! Otherwise, how will God judge the world? <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.7: But if by my lie God’s truth abounds to his glory, why am I also still being judged as a sinner? <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.8: And why not say, just as some people slanderously claim we say, “Let us do what is evil so that good may come”? <sup>ⓘ</sup> Their condemnation is deserved!
CSB17.9: What then? Are we any better off? <sup>[4]</sup> Not at all! For we have already charged that both Jews <sup>ⓙ</sup> and Gentiles <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> are all under sin, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.10: as it is written: There is no one righteous, not even one.
CSB17.11:
There is no one who understands;
there is no one who seeks God.
CSB17.12:
All have turned away;
all alike have become worthless.
There is no one who does what is good,
not even one. [7] ⓜ
CSB17.13:
Their throat is an open grave;
they deceive with their tongues. [8] ⓝ
Vipers’ venom is under their lips. [9] ⓐ
CSB17.14: Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness. [10] ⓑ
CSB17.15: Their feet are swift to shed blood;
CSB17.16: ruin and wretchedness are in their paths,
CSB17.17: and the path of peace they have not known. [11] ⓒ
CSB17.18: There is no fear of God before their eyes. [12] ⓓ
CSB17.19: Now we know that whatever the law says, <sup>ⓔ</sup> it speaks to those who are subject to the law, <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> so that every mouth may be shut and the whole world may become subject to God’s judgment. <sup>[14]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.20: For no one will be justified <sup>[15]</sup> in his sight by the works of the law, <sup>ⓗ</sup> because the knowledge of sin comes through the law. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.21: But now, apart from the law, the righteousness of God has been revealed, <sup>ⓙ</sup> attested by the Law and the Prophets. <sup>[16]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.22: The righteousness of God is through faith <sup>ⓛ</sup> in Jesus Christ <sup>[17]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup> to all who believe, <sup>ⓝ</sup> since there is no distinction. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: For all have sinned <sup>ⓑ</sup> and fall short of the <sup>[18]</sup> glory of God.
CSB17.24: They are justified freely by his grace <sup>ⓒ</sup> through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.25: God presented him as an atoning sacrifice <sup>[19]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> in his blood, <sup>ⓕ</sup> received through faith, to demonstrate his righteousness, because in his restraint God <sup>ⓖ</sup> passed over the sins previously committed. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.26: God presented him to demonstrate his righteousness at the present time, so that he would be righteous and declare righteous <sup>[20]</sup> the one who has faith in Jesus.
CSB17.27: Where, then, is boasting? <sup>ⓘ</sup> It is excluded. By what kind of law? <sup>[21]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> By one of works? No, on the contrary, by a law <sup>[22]</sup> of faith.
CSB17.28: For we conclude that a person is justified by faith apart from the works of the law. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.29: Or is God the God of Jews only? <sup>ⓛ</sup> Is he not the God of Gentiles too? Yes, of Gentiles too,
CSB17.30: since there is one God <sup>ⓜ</sup> who will justify the circumcised by faith <sup>ⓝ</sup> and the uncircumcised through faith.
CSB17.31: Do we then nullify the law through faith? Absolutely not! <sup>ⓐ</sup> On the contrary, we uphold the law. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: Then what is the advantage of the Jew? Or what is the benefit of circumcision?
AMP.2: Great in every respect. To begin with, the Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God [His very words].
AMP.3: What then? If some did not believe or were unfaithful [to God], their lack of belief will not nullify and make invalid the faithfulness of God and His word, will it?
AMP.4:
Certainly not! Let God be found true [as He will be], though every person be found a liar, just as it is written [in Scripture],
"That You may be justified in Your words,
And prevail when You are judged [by sinful men]."
AMP.5: But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God, what shall we say? God is not wrong to inflict His wrath [on us], is He? (I am speaking in purely human terms.)
AMP.6: Certainly not! For otherwise, how will God judge the world?
AMP.7: But [as you might say] if through my lie God's truth was magnified and abounded to His glory, why am I still being judged as a sinner?
AMP.8: And why not say, (as some slanderously report and claim that we teach) "Let us do evil so that good may come of it"? Their condemnation [by God] is just.
AMP.9: Well then, are we [Jews] better off than they? Not at all; for we have already charged that both Jews and Greeks (Gentiles) are under the control of sin and subject to its power.
AMP.10:
As it is written and forever remains written,
"There is none righteous [none that meets God's standard], not even one.
AMP.11:
"There is none who understands,
There is none who seeks for God.
AMP.12:
"All have turned aside, together they have become useless;
There is none who does good, no, not one."
AMP.13:
"Their throat is an open grave;
They [habitually] deceive with their tongues."
"The venom of asps is beneath their lips."
AMP.14: "Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness."
AMP.15: "Their feet are swift to shed blood,
AMP.16: Destruction and misery are in their paths,
AMP.17: And they have not known the path of peace."
AMP.18: "There is no fear of God [and His awesome power] before their eyes."
AMP.19: Now we know that whatever the Law [of Moses] says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, so that [the excuses of] every mouth may be silenced [from protesting] and that all the world may be held accountable to God [and subject to His judgment].
AMP.20: For no person will be justified [freed of guilt and declared righteous] in His sight by [trying to do] the works of the Law. For through the Law we become conscious of sin [and the recognition of sin directs us toward repentance, but provides no remedy for sin].
AMP.21: But now the righteousness of God has been clearly revealed [independently and completely] apart from the Law, though it is [actually] confirmed by the Law and the [words and writings of the] Prophets.
AMP.22: This righteousness of God comes through faith in Jesus Christ for all those [Jew or Gentile] who believe [and trust in Him and acknowledge Him as God's Son]. There is no distinction,
AMP.23: since all have sinned and continually fall short of the glory of God,
AMP.24: and are being justified [declared free of the guilt of sin, made acceptable to God, and granted eternal life] as a gift by His [precious, undeserved] grace, through the redemption [the payment for our sin] which is [provided] in Christ Jesus,
AMP.25: whom God displayed publicly [before the eyes of the world] as a [life-giving] sacrifice of atonement and reconciliation (propitiation) by His blood [to be received] through faith. This was to demonstrate His righteousness [which demands punishment for sin], because in His forbearance [His deliberate restraint] He passed over the sins previously committed [before Jesus' crucifixion].
AMP.26: It was to demonstrate His righteousness at the present time, so that He would be just and the One who justifies those who have faith in Jesus [and rely confidently on Him as Savior].
AMP.27: Then what becomes of [our] boasting? It is excluded [entirely ruled out, banished]. On what principle? On [the principle of good] works? No, but on the principle of faith.
AMP.28: For we maintain that an individual is justified by faith distinctly apart from works of the Law [the observance of which has nothing to do with justification, that is, being declared free of the guilt of sin and made acceptable to God].
AMP.29: Or is God the God of Jews only? Is He not also the God of Gentiles [who were not given the Law]? Yes, of Gentiles also,
AMP.30: since indeed it is one [and the same] God who will justify the circumcised by faith [which began with Abraham] and the uncircumcised through [their newly acquired] faith.
AMP.31: Do we then nullify the Law by this faith [making the Law of no effect, overthrowing it]? Certainly not! On the contrary, we confirm and establish and uphold the Law [since it convicts us all of sin, pointing to the need for salvation].

MSG.1: So what difference does it make who's a Jew and who isn't, who has been trained in God's ways and who hasn't?
MSG.2: As it turns out, it makes a lot of difference—but not the difference so many have assumed. First, there's the matter of being put in charge of writing down and caring for God's revelation, these Holy Scriptures.
MSG.3: So, what if, in the course of doing that, some of those Jews abandoned their post? God didn't abandon them. Do you think their faithlessness cancels out his faithfulness?
MSG.4: Not on your life! Depend on it: God keeps his word even when the whole world is lying through its teeth. Scripture says the same: Your words stand fast and true; Rejection doesn't faze you.
MSG.5: But if our wrongdoing only underlines and confirms God's rightdoing, shouldn't we be commended for helping out? Since our bad words don't even make a dent in his good words, isn't it wrong of God to back us to the wall and hold us to our word? These questions come up.
MSG.6: The answer to such questions is no, a most emphatic No! How else would things ever get straightened out if God didn't do the straightening?
MSG.7: It's simply perverse to say, "If my lies serve to show off God's truth all the more gloriously, why blame me? I'm doing God a favor."
MSG.8: Some people are actually trying to put such words in our mouths, claiming that we go around saying, "The more evil we do, the more good God does, so let's just do it!" That's pure slander, as I'm sure you'll agree.
MSG.9: So where does that put us? Do we Jews get a better break than the others? Not really. Basically, all of us, whether insiders or outsiders, start out in identical conditions, which is to say that we all start out as sinners. Scripture leaves no doubt about it:
MSG.10: There's nobody living right, not even one,
MSG.11: nobody who knows the score, nobody alert for God.
MSG.12: They've all taken the wrong turn; they've all wandered down blind alleys. No one's living right; I can't find a single one.
MSG.13: Their throats are gaping graves, their tongues slick as mud slides. Every word they speak is tinged with poison.
MSG.14: They open their mouths and pollute the air.
MSG.15: They race for the honor of sinner-of-the-year,
MSG.16: litter the land with heartbreak and ruin,
MSG.17: Don't know the first thing about living with others.
MSG.18: They never give God the time of day.
MSG.19: This makes it clear, doesn't it, that whatever is written in these Scriptures is not what God says about others but to us to whom these Scriptures were addressed in the first place! And it's clear enough, isn't it, that we're sinners, every one of us, in the same sinking boat with everybody else?
MSG.20: Our involvement with God's revelation doesn't put us right with God. What it does is force us to face our complicity in everyone else's sin.
MSG.21: But in our time something new has been added. What Moses and the prophets witnessed to all those years has happened.
MSG.22: The God-setting-things-right that we read about has become Jesus-setting-things-right for us. And not only for us, but for everyone who believes in him. For there is no difference between us and them in this.
MSG.23: Since we've compiled this long and sorry record as sinners (both us and them) and proved that we are utterly incapable of living the glorious lives God wills for us,
MSG.24: God did it for us. Out of sheer generosity he put us in right standing with himself. A pure gift. He got us out of the mess we're in and restored us to where he always wanted us to be. And he did it by means of Jesus Christ.
MSG.25: God sacrificed Jesus on the altar of the world to clear that world of sin. Having faith in him sets us in the clear. God decided on this course of action in full view of the public—to set the world in the clear with himself through the sacrifice of Jesus, finally taking care of the sins he had so patiently endured.
MSG.26: This is not only clear, but it's now—this is current history! God sets things right. He also makes it possible for us to live in his rightness.
MSG.27: So where does that leave our proud Jewish insider claims and counterclaims? Canceled? Yes, canceled. What we've learned is this: God does not respond to what we do; we respond to what God does.
MSG.28: We've finally figured it out. Our lives get in step with God and all others by letting him set the pace, not by proudly or anxiously trying to run the parade.
MSG.29: And where does that leave our proud Jewish claim of having a corner on God? Also canceled. God is the God of outsider non-Jews as well as insider Jews.
MSG.30: How could it be otherwise since there is only one God? God sets right all who welcome his action and enter into it, both those who follow our religious system and those who have never heard of our religion.
MSG.31: But by shifting our focus from what we do to what God does, don't we cancel out all our careful keeping of the rules and ways God commanded? Not at all. What happens, in fact, is that by putting that entire way of life in its proper place, we confirm it.

NIV.1:
God’s Faithfulness
What advantage, then, is there in being a Jew, or what value is there in circumcision?
NIV.2: Much in every way! First of all, they have been entrusted with the very words of God.
NIV.3: What if some did not have faith? Will their lack of faith nullify God’s faithfulness?
NIV.4:
Not at all! Let God be true, and every man a liar. As it is written:
“So that you may be proved right when you speak
and prevail when you judge.”
NIV.5: But if our unrighteousness brings out God’s righteousness more clearly, what shall we say? That God is unjust in bringing his wrath on us? (I am using a human argument.)
NIV.6: Certainly not! If that were so, how could God judge the world?
NIV.7: Someone might argue, “If my falsehood enhances God’s truthfulness and so increases his glory, why am I still condemned as a sinner?”
NIV.8: Why not say—as we are being slanderously reported as saying and as some claim that we say—“Let us do evil that good may result”? Their condemnation is deserved.
NIV.9:
No One Is Righteous
What shall we conclude then? Are we any better? Not at all! We have already made the charge that Jews and Gentiles alike are all under sin.
NIV.10:
As it is written:
“There is no one righteous, not even one;
NIV.11:
there is no one who understands,
no one who seeks God.
NIV.12:
All have turned away,
they have together become worthless;
there is no one who does good,
not even one.”
NIV.13:
“Their throats are open graves;
their tongues practice deceit.”
“The poison of vipers is on their lips.”
NIV.14: “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
NIV.15: “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
NIV.16: ruin and misery mark their ways,
NIV.17: and the way of peace they do not know.”
NIV.18: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
NIV.19: Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced and the whole world held accountable to God.
NIV.20: Therefore no one will be declared righteous in his sight by observing the law; rather, through the law we become conscious of sin.
NIV.21:
Righteousness Through Faith
But now a righteousness from God, apart from law, has been made known, to which the Law and the Prophets testify.
NIV.22: This righteousness from God comes through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe. There is no difference,
NIV.23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
NIV.24: and are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that came by Christ Jesus.
NIV.25: God presented him as a sacrifice of atonement, through faith in his blood. He did this to demonstrate his justice, because in his forbearance he had left the sins committed beforehand unpunished—
NIV.26: he did it to demonstrate his justice at the present time, so as to be just and the one who justifies those who have faith in Jesus.
NIV.27: Where, then, is boasting? It is excluded. On what principle? On that of observing the law? No, but on that of faith.
NIV.28: For we maintain that a man is justified by faith apart from observing the law.
NIV.29: Is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles too? Yes, of Gentiles too,
NIV.30: since there is only one God, who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through that same faith.
NIV.31: Do we, then, nullify the law by this faith? Not at all! Rather, we uphold the law.

NRSVCE.1: Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
NRSVCE.2: Much, in every way. For in the first place the Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God.
NRSVCE.3: What if some were unfaithful? Will their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
NRSVCE.4:
By no means! Although everyone is a liar, let God be proved true, as it is written, “So that you may be justified in your words,
and prevail in your judging.”
NRSVCE.5: But if our injustice serves to confirm the justice of God, what should we say? That God is unjust to inflict wrath on us? (I speak in a human way.)
NRSVCE.6: By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
NRSVCE.7: But if through my falsehood God’s truthfulness abounds to his glory, why am I still being condemned as a sinner?
NRSVCE.8: And why not say (as some people slander us by saying that we say), “Let us do evil so that good may come”? Their condemnation is deserved!
NRSVCE.9: What then? Are we any better off? No, not at all; for we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks, are under the power of sin,
NRSVCE.10: as it is written: “There is no one who is righteous, not even one;
NRSVCE.11:
there is no one who has understanding,
there is no one who seeks God.
NRSVCE.12:
All have turned aside, together they have become worthless;
there is no one who shows kindness,
there is not even one.”
NRSVCE.13:
“Their throats are opened graves;
they use their tongues to deceive.”
“The venom of vipers is under their lips.”
NRSVCE.14: “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
NRSVCE.15: “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
NRSVCE.16: ruin and misery are in their paths,
NRSVCE.17: and the way of peace they have not known.”
NRSVCE.18: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
NRSVCE.19: Now we know that whatever the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced, and the whole world may be held accountable to God.
NRSVCE.20: For “no human being will be justified in his sight” by deeds prescribed by the law, for through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
NRSVCE.21: But now, apart from law, the righteousness of God has been disclosed, and is attested by the law and the prophets,
NRSVCE.22: the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction,
NRSVCE.23: since all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God;
NRSVCE.24: they are now justified by his grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.25: whom God put forward as a sacrifice of atonement by his blood, effective through faith. He did this to show his righteousness, because in his divine forbearance he had passed over the sins previously committed;
NRSVCE.26: it was to prove at the present time that he himself is righteous and that he justifies the one who has faith in Jesus.
NRSVCE.27: Then what becomes of boasting? It is excluded. By what law? By that of works? No, but by the law of faith.
NRSVCE.28: For we hold that a person is justified by faith apart from works prescribed by the law.
NRSVCE.29: Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
NRSVCE.30: since God is one; and he will justify the circumcised on the ground of faith and the uncircumcised through that same faith.
NRSVCE.31: Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary, we uphold the law.

NET.1: Therefore what advantage does the Jew have, or what is the value of circumcision?
NET.2: Actually, there are many advantages. First of all, the Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God.
NET.3: What then? If some did not believe, does their unbelief nullify the faithfulness of God?
NET.4: Absolutely not! Let God be proven true, and every human being shown up as a liar, just as it is written: “so that you will be justified in your words and will prevail when you are judged.”
NET.5: But if our unrighteousness demonstrates the righteousness of God, what shall we say? The God who inflicts wrath is not unrighteous, is he? (I am speaking in human terms.)
NET.6: Absolutely not! For otherwise how could God judge the world?
NET.7: For if by my lie the truth of God enhances his glory, why am I still actually being judged as a sinner?
NET.8: And why not say, “Let us do evil so that good may come of it”? — as some who slander us allege that we say. (Their condemnation is deserved!)
NET.9: What then? Are we better off? Certainly not, for we have already charged that Jews and Greeks alike are all under sin,
NET.10: just as it is written: “There is no one righteous, not even one,
NET.11: there is no one who understands, there is no one who seeks God.
NET.12: All have turned away, together they have become worthless; there is no one who shows kindness, not even one.”
NET.13: “Their throats are open graves, they deceive with their tongues, the poison of asps is under their lips.”
NET.14: “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
NET.15: “Their feet are swift to shed blood,
NET.16: ruin and misery are in their paths,
NET.17: and the way of peace they have not known.”
NET.18: “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
NET.19: Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be silenced and the whole world may be held accountable to God.
NET.20: For no one is declared righteous before him by the works of the law, for through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
NET.21: But now apart from the law the righteousness of God (which is attested by the law and the prophets) has been disclosed —
NET.22: namely, the righteousness of God through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction,
NET.23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
NET.24: But they are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus.
NET.25: God publicly displayed him at his death as the mercy seat accessible through faith. This was to demonstrate his righteousness, because God in his forbearance had passed over the sins previously committed.
NET.26: This was also to demonstrate his righteousness in the present time, so that he would be just and the justifier of the one who lives because of Jesus’ faithfulness.
NET.27: Where, then, is boasting? It is excluded! By what principle? Of works? No, but by the principle of faith!
NET.28: For we consider that a person is declared righteous by faith apart from the works of the law.
NET.29: Or is God the God of the Jews only? Is he not the God of the Gentiles too? Yes, of the Gentiles too!
NET.30: Since God is one, he will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
NET.31: Do we then nullify the law through faith? Absolutely not! Instead we uphold the law.
